steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent by declaring instructions, actions, and knowledge.
Type  ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}}  into the "Input an application name" text field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension popup within Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to confirm the application name ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}} in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the input in the "Create a name for Copilot connector" text field within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Type  ${{var:connection_id:gc#####}}  into the "add a connection ID for Copilot connector" text field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension popup within Visual Studio Code.
Type text: '${{var:connection_id:gc#####}} ' in the input field labeled "Add a connection ID" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface. Press 'Enter' to confirm or 'Escape' to cancel.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the input "test001" in the "Add a connection ID" field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Default folder" input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project root folder path.
@assertion there's "Preview README.md" tab header in the Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Close" option in the context menu of a tab within Visual Studio Code to close the currently active file or tab.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
# Define file path
file_path="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_rqPAE/env/.env.dev"

# Check if the file exists
if [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
    # Replace CONNECTOR_REPOS and CONNECTOR_ID values
    sed -i "s/^CONNECTOR_REPOS=.*/CONNECTOR_REPOS='microsoft\/Agent365-Samples'/" "$file_path"
    sed -i "s/^CONNECTOR_ID=.*/CONNECTOR_ID='gcEqEUD'/" "$file_path"
    echo "Values updated successfully in $file_path."
else
    echo "Error: File $file_path does not exist."
    exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Values updated successfully in /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_rqPAE/env/.env.dev.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code fill into /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/env/.env.local, replace CONNECTOR_REPOS= 'microsoft/Agent365-Samples' and save the file.
Click at position (20, 69)
Click on the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the ".env.local" file in the "env" folder within the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to open it.
@assertion CONNECTOR_REPOS has value
Click the tab for ".env.local" in the Visual Studio Code editor, directly beneath the top navigation bar, to activate the file view for editing environment variables.
Click the ".env.local" file in the "env" folder within the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug" dropdown menu in the "RUN AND DEBUG" panel of the Visual Studio Code interface, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (137, 55).

Click the "Install" button in the Azure Functions extension recommendation popup within the Visual Studio Code workspace. The button is located at the lower right of the interface, beneath the popup message asking if you want to install the recommended extension.
Click the "Cancel" button in the error dialog that states "Could not find the task 'func: host start'" within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, with the “Preview WBD-hybrid announcement.md” file active in the editor area.
Type the text 'reload window' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the application interface.
Press the Enter key to select the "Developer: Reload Window" command in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.

Click the "Extensions" icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to open the Extensions panel. The red crosshair marks this icon, which is visually represented by four squares forming a cross, located in the left-most vertical toolbar.
Click the "Debug" dropdown button in the RUN AND DEBUG panel of Visual Studio Code, located in the upper left sidebar of the workspace.
@assertion there has 'Start' and 'npm' and 'func' task is exist
